canonical_title: OpenAI signs 25-year Georgia power deal for 3.2-gigawatt data center
treatment: Treat the signed power agreement, capacity, cost commitments, demand-response provision, and local investment package as covered. Reinclude only for construction approval, material schedule/capacity change, financing change, grid or ratepayer impact, cancellation, or operational commissioning.
known_facts: Georgia Power signed a 25-year power-supply agreement with OpenAI for Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia.
The planned data center requires about 3.2 gigawatts, with phased delivery expected between 2028 and 2032.
OpenAI agreed to pay the full infrastructure and electric-service costs and to provide up to 1 gigawatt back to the grid through curtailment during peak demand.
OpenAI announced an $80 million community investment fund and up to $71 million in Codex credits for Georgia colleges, universities, and technical schools.
reinclude_only_if: The project receives or loses a material permit, financing commitment, or construction milestone.
Capacity, delivery dates, grid-curtailment terms, ratepayer protections, or project cost materially change.
The project is delayed, cancelled, commissioned, or causes a documented grid or community impact.
notes: Selected as a concrete AI infrastructure hard delta within the post-night-report window.
